title: What were the reviews like in EoE
display_title: What were the reviews like in EoE
wikitext:
[]: What were the reviews like in EoE?             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 7:46 am I'm curious, here in the forums, what did the fans think of EoE? I want to make a comparison of the reviews from that movie and the reviews of Eva Q.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 11:24 am Speaking for myself, I thought End of Evangelion finished the series well when taken in conjunction with episodes 25 and 26. It was a bit of a shock to the system when I first watched it, but I really enjoyed it. There's been quite a bit of discussion on the topic in the forums, and I've seen a really wide range of opinions. I don't think it would be too hard to find relevant topics if you search the Discussion subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by Vicious » Mon Jan 14, 2013 11:32 am It's only the pinnacle of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Jan 14, 2013 11:44 am Personally When I first watched it I had to stop after Asuka's final fight, but after a while I grew to love it. It quickly became my favorite animated film and it became the standard by which I judge all anime films. BTW The search option is you're friend. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 14, 2013 12:15 pm The Evangelion mailing list might have some turn-of-the-century reactions. Getting Japanese reactions at the time may require some digging.

[]:             Quote Post by Evanut » Mon Jan 14, 2013 12:28 pm My first thoughts after seeing EOE were along the lines of 'terrifying, impossible to understand, exceedingly emotional, and unexpected'. I liked it more than the original TV series ending from the beginning and the film only got better with subsequent viewings. The first time I saw it I'd rate it a 8-9, second time and since, a 10. now it sounds more like you wanted to know what the fans thought at the time. I only saw the movie a few years ago, so I'm not the person to ask. I would guess the reviews were just as mixed as 3.0 even though EOE is miles better as a film.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 1:19 pm Evanut wrote: now it sounds more like you wanted to know what the fans thought at the time. I only saw the movie a few years ago, so I'm not the person to ask. I would guess the reviews were just as mixed as 3.0 even though EOE is miles better as a film.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 14, 2013 9:07 pm EoE was the biggest mindfuck that my young mind ever saw. Older, wiser me thinks that it was one of the best anime movies of all time, sitting just above Ghost in the Shell. Older, wiser me still thinks of it as mindfuckery beyond all sane mindfuckery. 3.0 was more of the same, except more mind-boggling. I didn't even think that it would be possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Jan 14, 2013 9:48 pm I wouldn't just check here for reactions. If it's a forum that means that it's the more dedicated fans that will be giving you their opinions. See if you can find reactions from more general viewers too. As for me, I'd say painful. Not that it was bad, but that it hurt to see the charecters go through some of what they did. It's actually the only thing for original Eva I don't own, because I knew I'd probably have a hard time ever watching it again. Also my initial reactions were ugly. But mostly because it's painful. I hated seeing Asuka get butchered. I hated that in the end Shinji was powerless to do anything to the things that killed her. I really hated our grand conclusion being another brain episode, though I'm over that now. And I'm always saddened by the fact I believe that not quite everyone can return, and that Rei and Kaworu are going to have to stay gone. And then I get into endor holocaust territory... You know what I'm going to stop now.

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 10:58 pm Wow, seems like it was tough. Seeing as I was a little kid back then, I probably would be confused at turn my screen off in frustration and just give up on everything. I can see now why Evangelion is popular. It held on to time. Not many series can do that. Most series these days are just one day popcorn flick that you will casually go to if your feeling just a little rememberance, but Evangelion has managed to conjure up freaking articles on the philosophical about it. In the end it turned out that it was a good thing that EoE had the mind fuck ending. If it didn't, it wouldn't hold standard of time and everyone would just forget about it. Incredible how these things work.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Jan 15, 2013 7:00 am You could also look into the old discussion threads and the posts of a "Symbv" user, since he apparently was in Japan at the time, went to the theaters during the first screenings of EoE, and wrote a bit about what it was like in the forums. As for me, it was a really great and proper end for NGE. Very clear, honest and beautiful.
